channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge gives Trump administration two days to release billions of dollars in blocked foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday gave the Trump administration less than two days to release billions of dollars in U.S. foreign aid, saying the administration had given no sign of complying with his nearly two-week-old court order to ease its funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass budget resolution, clearing a key early test for Trump agenda
item.description: The GOP framework that passed Tuesday allows the party to move ahead on a sweeping multitrillion plan to address defense, energy, immigration and tax policy.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: Trump budget bill with $4.5 trillion in tax cuts survives House vote
item.description: House Republicans are working through disagreements to come together on a massive piece of legislation to advance President Donald Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House confirms who was appointed as acting administrator of DOGE
item.description: White House officials told Fox News the acting administrator of DOGE is Amy Gleason despite billionaire Elon Musk overseeing the department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Teachers union sues over Trump administration's deadline to end school diversity programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new federal lawsuit in Maryland is challenging a Trump administration memo giving the nation’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate “race-based” practices of any kind or risk losing their federal money.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he will offer 'gold cards' for $5 million path to citizenship, replacing investor visas
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he plans to offer a “gold card” visa with a path to citizenship for $5 million, replacing a 35-year-old visa for investors.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal employees may get more demands to justify their work at Elon Musk's direction
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The turmoil that enveloped the federal workforce over the last few days is unlikely to cease anytime soon as the U.S. government's human resources agency considers how to fulfill Elon Musk ’s demands.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southern border state governor takes on cartels and security with signing of executive order
item.description: Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs signed an executive order this week to establish a multi-agency task force to take on cartels and secure the border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Upheaval and firings at CDC raise fears about disease outbreak response
item.description: Staff and observers worry that the agency may not be prepared for emerging threats including bird flu and insect-borne diseases.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Prosecutors conclude Army veteran's Capitol riot pardon also covers a separate weapons case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has concluded that a military veteran's presidential pardon for charges that he stormed the U.S. Capitol also extends to his separate conviction for illegally possessing stolen grenades and classified information, according to a court filing Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Singer-musician Rhiannon Giddens calls off Kennedy Center show, citing Trump takeover
item.description: Award-winning singe-musician Rhiannon Giddens has become the latest artist to call off an appearance at the Kennedy Center, which has been in upheaval since President Donald Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: Hillel Italie, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nevada Democrat proposes study on slavery's impact, possible reparations
item.description: A Nevada Democratic assemblymember is proposing a bill to create a commission to study discrimination against Black Americans and possible reparations.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump administration's sweeping freeze on federal funding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge agreed Tuesday to continue blocking President Donald Trump's administration from freezing grants and loans potentially totaling trillions of dollars. U.S. District Judge Loren AliKhan in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House to take over press pool amid 'Gulf of America' fight with AP
item.description: The White House said Tuesday that it would take over which outlets are allowed into the press pool covering the president, wrestling such control from the White House Correspondents' Association.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court rules against drivers in a case advocates say could make civil-rights claims harder
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court said Tuesday that people who win early rulings in civil rights cases won't necessarily be able to recover their legal fees, a finding that both conservative and liberal groups had argued could make it harder to fight for people's rights in court.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal watchdog agency intervenes in Trump’s purge of probationary employees
item.description: U.S. Special Counsel Hampton Dellinger has asked the Merit Systems Protection Board to temporarily reinstate six federal employees fired from their jobs and is considering ways to seek relief for others.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: The petition calls on Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to cancel Musk's citizenship and Canadian passport.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: House Republicans adopts budget after Trump whips GOP holdouts
item.description: House Republican leadership is working to pass a budget resolution on Tuesday to pave the way for President Trump's agenda, but opposition has emerged from all sides of the conference.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Smog checks every 2 years good enough under new proposal at Nevada Legislature
item.description: Smog checks would be required once every two years instead of annually under legislation sponsored by a Democrat and a Republican in the Nevada Senate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hegseth says he fired the top military lawyers because they weren't well suited for the jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Monday that he was replacing the top lawyers for the military services because he didn't think they were “well-suited” to provide recommendations when lawful orders are given.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans’ love/hate relationship with the Education Department
item.description: President Trump has made clear he wants to close the U.S. Department of Education, but Republicans seem torn on just how far to go.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Roosevelt Hotel to shut down as migrant arrival center: NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – The migrant arrival center at the Roosevelt Hotel in Manhattan is set to shut down, New York City Mayor Eric Adams will announce on Monday. More than 173,000 migrants have gone through the site since it opened in May 2023, according to city data.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Proposed bill may criminalize landlords, property management for unpaid utilities
item.description: Jackson says he was inspired to write the bill after dilapidated apartments, like the Jolie and Woodlawn Terrace Apartments, forced law-abiding tenants out of their homes.
item.dc:creator: Myriam Samake, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers sue Elon Musk over email ultimatum
item.description: The lawsuit seeks to block mass layoffs, including any connected to an email distributed by the Office of Personnel Management on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump‘s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A U.S. district judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the administration from terminating or changing federal contracts they consider equity-related.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Union workers written up, fired for using sick days; Nevada lawmaker moves to close loophole
item.description: Some union workers are afraid to use sick days because they might be written up or fired for being "undependable," and now a Nevada lawmaker is coming to their aid.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dexter's Law: Pushing for tougher sentencing for animal cruelty
item.description: One Tampa Bay area lawmaker is pushing to toughen sentences for animal cruelty, specifically in cases like the shocking discovery of Dexter, a decapitated dog found at Fort DeSoto Beach.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge cancels trial for NYC Mayor Eric Adams but leaves corruption charges intact for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cancelled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Proposed $500,000 fund would help victims of investment schemes in Nevada
item.description: Victims of investment schemes would be eligible for $25,000 from a fund managed by the Nevada Secretary of State's Office under a proposal presented in Carson City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Cortez Masto, Rosen introduce proposal to block Trump plan to end birthright citizenship
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen introduced a proposal Thursday to block President Donald Trump's attempt to end the constitutional right to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling controversial LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC
